{"step_by_step_thinking": "The goal of the study is to compare the fat content in the expressed breast milk of pre-term infants to term infants. A longitudinal study is best suited for this purpose because it follows the same subjects over time, allowing for the collection of data on the fat content of breast milk from both pre-term and term infants as they grow. This design would enable the researchers to observe changes in fat content over time and make direct comparisons. A case-control study would not be appropriate as it compares individuals with a condition to those without, rather than following the same individuals over time. A prospective cohort study could be used, but the term 'Prospective coho' is not a recognized study design. An ambispective study is not suitable as it combines elements of both prospective and retrospective designs, which is not necessary for this specific study.", "answer_choice": "C"}
